ogTitle: Porepunkah vs Port Campbell: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Porepunkah and Port Campbell.
heading: Porepunkah vs Port Campbell — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Porepunkah, Victoria and Port Campbell, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Campbell has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Porepunkah (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Porepunkah or Port Campbell safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Porepunkah has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Port Campbell scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Porepunkah scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Port Campbell scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Porepunkah or Port Campbell?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Campbell has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
